name: card_image_lt_of_exists_distinct_same_image
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.FiniteSet

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem card_image_lt_of_exists_distinct_same_image
    {α β : Type*} [DecidableEq β]
    (s : Finset α) (f : α → β) {a b : α}
    (ha : a ∈ s) (hb : b ∈ s) (hab : a ≠ b) (hfab : f a = f b) :
    (s.image f).card < s.card := by
informal_prefix: /-- Finite-set induction bookkeeping for Mochizuki Lemma 2.2: if a map identifies two distinct elements of a finite set, the image has smaller cardinality. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: scherr_zieve_no_finite_cover_by_proper_subspaces
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.LinearAlgebra

open Set

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem scherr_zieve_no_finite_cover_by_proper_subspaces
    {K V : Type*} [DivisionRing K] [Infinite K] [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]
    (s : Finset (Subspace K V))
    (hproper : ∀ W ∈ s, W ≠ ⊤) :
    (⋃ W ∈ s, (W : Set V)) ≠ Set.univ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Scherr-Zieve Lemma 2.2 linear algebra: over an infinite field, finitely many proper subspaces cannot cover the whole vector space. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: scherr_zieve_exists_vector_avoiding_finite_proper_subspaces
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.LinearAlgebra

open Set

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem scherr_zieve_exists_vector_avoiding_finite_proper_subspaces
    {K V : Type*} [DivisionRing K] [Infinite K] [AddCommGroup V] [Module K V]
    (s : Finset (Subspace K V))
    (hproper : ∀ W ∈ s, W ≠ ⊤) :
    ∃ v : V, ∀ W ∈ s, v ∉ W := by
informal_prefix: /-- Scherr-Zieve Lemma 2.2 avoidance form: choose a vector outside every subspace in a finite family of proper subspaces over an infinite field. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: lorscheid_compact_image
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.Topology

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem lorscheid_compact_image
    {X Y : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] [TopologicalSpace Y]
    {s : Set X} {f : X → Y}
    (hs : IsCompact s)
    (hf : Continuous f) :
    IsCompact (f '' s) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Local compactness layer: continuous images of compact sets are compact. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: compact_elim_finite_subcover_indexed
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.Topology

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem compact_elim_finite_subcover_indexed
    {X ι : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X]
    {s : Set X} {b : Set ι} {c : ι → Set X}
    (hs : IsCompact s)
    (hcopen : ∀ i ∈ b, IsOpen (c i))
    (hcover : s ⊆ ⋃ i ∈ b, c i) :
    ∃ b' : Set ι, b' ⊆ b ∧ Set.Finite b' ∧ s ⊆ ⋃ i ∈ b', c i := by
informal_prefix: /-- Compactness layer: every indexed open cover of a compact set has a finite subcover. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

theorem projective_line_zero_ne_infinity
    (K : Type*) [DivisionRing K] :
    HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.zero K ≠
      HilbertTest.SourceStack.ProjectiveLine.infinity K := by
informal_prefix: /-- Projective-line source layer: the points 0=[0:1] and infinity=[1:0] are distinct. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: finset_complex_inf_norm_sub_pos
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem finset_complex_inf_norm_sub_pos
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℂ) (hS : S.Nonempty) (hβ : β ∉ S) :
    0 < S.inf' hS (fun α : ℂ => ‖α - β‖) := by
informal_prefix: /-- Complex finite-set source layer: the nearest-point distance from β to a finite set not containing β is strictly positive. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_complex_point_nearby_separating_finset
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_complex_point_nearby_separating_finset
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℂ) (C : ℝ) (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : β ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℂ,
      lam ≠ β ∧
      (∀ α ∈ S, lam ≠ α) ∧
      ∀ α ∈ S, C * ‖β - lam‖ ≤ ‖α - lam‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 analytic source layer: choose a pole lam near β so every point of the finite set is C times farther from lam than β is. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_reciprocal_translate_separating_finset
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_reciprocal_translate_separating_finset
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℂ) (C : ℝ) (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : β ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℂ,
      lam ≠ β ∧
      (∀ α ∈ S, lam ≠ α) ∧
      ∀ α ∈ S,
        C * ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam α‖ ≤
          ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate lam β‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 reciprocal-map source layer: after choosing a pole lam, the map z ↦ 1/(z-lam) separates β from the finite set by factor C. Use the source-stack theorem. -/

name: exists_rational_reciprocal_translate_separating_finset
header:
import HilbertTest.SourceStack.ComplexSeparation

namespace HilbertTest.HilbertDataset
formal_statement:
theorem exists_rational_reciprocal_translate_separating_finset
    (S : Finset ℂ) (β : ℚ) (C : ℝ) (hC : 0 < C) (hβ : (β : ℂ) ∉ S) :
    ∃ lam : ℚ,
      (lam : ℂ) ≠ (β : ℂ) ∧
      (∀ α ∈ S, (lam : ℂ) ≠ α) ∧
      ∀ α ∈ S,
        C * ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate (lam : ℂ) α‖ ≤
          ‖HilbertTest.SourceStack.reciprocalTranslate (lam : ℂ) (β : ℂ)‖ := by
informal_prefix: /-- Mochizuki Lemma 2.3 rational refinement: if β is rational, choose the reciprocal-map pole lam rational while keeping the same finite-set separation estimate. Use the source-stack theorem. -/
